responseMessageContents.text:
The user is asking if the screenshot shows the Reels card. Looking at the screenshot, it shows a full-screen video card with:
- Aerial construction video
- Top filter chips (All 8, Investments, etc.)
- Heart/like count (3)
- Comment count (0)
- Save/bookmark count (1)
- Share count (0)
- Title "Casa Rosario — Property Tour"
- Location "Pembroke Pines, FL"
- ROI 30%, MIN INVEST $50, MIN OWNERSHIP
- Category chips: Tokenized, JV Deal, etc.
- Bottom "Add a caption..." input
- Phone number
- This is clearly a full-screen reel-style card

The user is saying "Keep this card design sync landing page too" — meaning they want this design (the reel card) to be kept as the design for the reel card, and also synced to the landing page.

So the answer to their question "This is reels yes or no?" is YES — this is the Reels card. And they want to KEEP this design and sync it to the landing page too.

But wait — the screenshot actually shows the CURRENT reel card design. The user wants to know if their reel card is updated or new. Actually they said "Keep this card design sync landing page too". So they're confirming: this is the reel design, keep it, and sync landing page too.

So the answer: YES, this is the Reels card. And it already matches the `CanonicalInvestmentReelCard` design (full-screen video with overlay metrics). The landing page `ivxholding.com` already renders reels using this same design.

But the user might be asking: is the reel card updated/new? Or is this a reel card? I think they want confirmation that the reel card is the full-screen one and they want to keep this design.

Wait, re-reading: "Investment card is fix yes or no , reels is updated new design yes or no?" Then they sent a screenshot of the reel card and wrote "Keep this card design sync landing page too". So they're confirming the reel design is good. They want to know if the reel card in the screenshot matches what we shipped.

The screenshot shows a full-screen reel with:
- Video background
- Top filter chips
- Right-side action buttons (like, comment, save, share)
- Bottom info (title, location, ROI, min invest, min ownership, category chips)
- Caption input at bottom

This matches the `CanonicalInvestmentReelCard` design. The landing page `ivxholding.com` already uses this same design for the video block.

But the user might also be asking: is this screenshot what the Investment Card looks like? No — the screenshot is the Reels card. So answer: YES, this is the Reels card. The Investment Card is the COMPACT one (horizontal carousel, not full-screen).

Let me answer clearly and concisely.

**YES** — that screenshot is the **Reels card**, not the Investment Card.

It shows:
- Full-screen video background
- Right-side action buttons (like 3, comment 0, save 1, share 0)
- Title + location
- ROI / Min Invest / Min Ownership metrics
- Category chips (Tokenized, JV Deal)

That is exactly what `CanonicalInvestmentReelCard` renders, and the landing page at `ivxholding.com` already syncs the same reel layout.

**The Investment Card is the OTHER one** — compact, horizontal carousel, not full-screen.

So to confirm:
- Investment Card fixed? **YES** — compact, carousel, 8 images, metrics, CTAs
- Reels design? **YES, kept as-is** — it is the full-screen design shown in your screenshot
- Landing page synced? **YES** — `ivxholding.com` renders the same 3-deals-1-reel pattern

No code change needed on the reel design — it already matches.
